reviewer.evidencePolicy: Only mark evidenceStatus=satisfied for fixture-runtime eval claims covered by focused CLI tests, a passing live fixture-runtime eval-test run, emitted packet hashes, and an eval-evaluate recheck of the observed packet.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names CLI routing and packet output behavior that is covered by focused smoke tests plus a live fixture-runtime command.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused CLI tests and a live `cautilus eval test --runtime fixture` run that produced eval-cases, eval-observed, and eval-summary packets while using the adapter-owned fixture backend.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep fixture runtime positioned as command-routing and packet-evaluation proof, not live product-runner proof.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names eval-test packet and proof metadata behavior that is visible in generated JSON packets.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by a live fixture-runtime eval-test run whose eval-observed and eval-summary packets both record proof.runtime=fixture, proof.proofClass=fixture-smoke, proof.proofClassSource=runtime, and proof.targetSurface=dev/skill.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep proof metadata assertions covered when changing eval packet schemas or runner readiness projection.

claimUpdates.whyThisLayer: The claim names the eval-evaluate no-runner boundary, which is covered by focused smoke tests and a recheck summary generated from an observed packet.
claimUpdates.evidenceStatusReason: Verified by focused CLI smoke tests and `cautilus eval evaluate --input eval-observed.json`, which produced an accept-now recheck summary from the observed packet without launching the adapter runner.
claimUpdates.nextAction: Keep eval-evaluate tests separate from runner launch tests so packet-only evaluation remains auditable.
